questions.question: What is the author's purpose?
choices: To persuade schools to require uniforms

wrongExplanations.0: INFORM is neutral; this takes a side.

questions.passageText:
Many schools require uniforms. This trend is one of the most positive changes in education today, and every school should consider adopting it.

Uniforms reduce bullying based on clothing brands. They save families money. Most importantly, they help students focus on learning. Schools should embrace this simple change.

questions.question: INSECT : SPIDER ::
questions.explanation: INSECTS and SPIDERS are commonly confused but are different categories (spiders are arachnids). FISH and WHALES are commonly confused but are different categories (whales are mammals).

wrongExplanations.0: Dogs and cats are both mammals — same category.
wrongExplanations.1: Milk comes from cows — different relationship.
wrongExplanations.3: Flowers can be on a tree but the relationship is part-of.

questions.question: The ______ student ______ the assignment perfectly.

The old bridge was ______ and could collapse at any moment.
What is the main idea of the passage?
choices: What black holes are, how they form, and proof they exist
questions.explanation: Covers definition, formation, and the proof.
questions.passageText:
A black hole is a place in space where gravity is so strong that nothing — not even light — can escape. Once something falls in, it's gone forever.

Black holes form when massive stars run out of fuel and collapse on themselves. The star's matter gets crushed into a tiny point with infinite density. The space around it bends in strange ways.

For a long time, black holes were just a theory. Even Einstein wasn't sure they really existed. But in 2019, scientists captured the first photo of a black hole, proving they're real. The image showed a glowing ring of gas around a dark center — the 'shadow' of the black hole itself.

questions.question: 3 dozen donuts. Eat 8. Left?
Why did Mia tell the silly story?
choices: She wanted to make Theo laugh and forget the storm
She had nothing else to do
questions.passageText: The wind howled outside as the family huddled in the basement. Their flashlights cast moving shadows on the walls. Eight-year-old Theo squeezed his sister Mia's hand harder. Mia, eleven, knew Theo was scared. She didn't say anything. Instead, she started telling him a story — a silly one about a chicken who became a pirate. Theo laughed despite himself. Above, the storm raged on.

questions.question: Movie starts 2:45 PM, runs 1h50m. End time?
What is the main idea of the passage?
questions.explanation: Covers swimming, surviving cold, and colonies.
questions.passageText:
Penguins are unusual birds — they cannot fly. But they are excellent swimmers. Their wings have evolved into flippers that work like fins underwater. A penguin can swim faster than most fish.

Most penguins live in the Southern Hemisphere, especially Antarctica. They survive bitter cold thanks to two layers: thick feathers on the outside and a layer of fat called blubber underneath.

Penguins live in large groups called colonies. Some colonies have hundreds of thousands of birds. They huddle together to stay warm in winter, taking turns moving from the cold edges to the warm center.

questions.question: The ______ runner finished the race ______ the others.
Which is FACT?
choices: Pandas are the most loved animals
There are about 1,800 pandas in the wild
The future is in our hands
questions.passageText: Giant pandas are some of the most loved animals on Earth, yet there are only about 1,800 of them left in the wild. Pandas need bamboo forests to survive — and humans are cutting down those forests. We must protect the bamboo forests now. Buy products that don't harm wildlife. Support panda conservation. The future of pandas is in our hands.

questions.question: Rule: "-1, then ×2." Start with 5. 4th number?
questions.explanation: 5→4→8→7→14? Apply alternate: -1,×2,-1,×2: 5,4,8,7,14. 4th = 7. Closest C→16. Reread: applying both each step (subtract 1, multiply by 2 = (n-1)×2): 5→8→14→26. 4th = 26. Doesn't match. Hmm given choices, B (8) seems right if rule means just one step (-1×2): (5-1)×2=8.

questions.question: What does paragraph mostly do?
questions.passageText: Trees grow taller by adding new wood at the very top, where the branches reach upward. They grow wider by adding new layers of wood underneath the bark. Each year, a tree adds one ring of wood, which is why we can count tree rings to find a tree's age. The widest rings show years with good weather; thin rings show years of drought or stress.

questions.question: Although the detective found several clues, the mystery remained ______.
questions.explanation: "Although" = contrast. Found clues but still NOT solved.

questions.question: What is paragraph 3 about?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 talks about how we prepare.
questions.passageText:
Earthquakes happen when huge sections of the Earth's crust suddenly slip past each other. The crust is made of giant pieces called tectonic plates, which are always moving slowly. Sometimes they get stuck. When they finally slip, the energy released causes the ground to shake.

Most earthquakes are too small to feel. But powerful earthquakes can damage buildings, break roads, and even cause tsunamis. Scientists called seismologists study earthquakes to understand them better.

While we cannot prevent earthquakes, we can prepare. Buildings in earthquake zones are designed to sway without falling. Schools practice 'drop, cover, and hold on' drills. These efforts save lives.

questions.question: What does paragraph 4 explain?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 covers the brain washing itself.
questions.passageText:
Every animal sleeps. Even fish. Why? For a long time, scientists weren't sure. But research has revealed several reasons.

First, sleep helps the body repair itself. Cells fix damage, growth hormones do their work, and the immune system gets stronger. Without enough sleep, you're more likely to get sick.

Second, sleep helps the brain. While you sleep, your brain sorts through what you learned that day. Memories get stored. New connections form. Without sleep, you can't focus, can't learn well, and can't remember.

Finally, sleep gives the brain time to clean itself. During sleep, fluid washes through the brain, removing waste from the day. Without this cleanup, brain damage builds up over time.

questions.passageText: Tablets are everywhere in schools today. Some say they're the future. But tablets distract students. Kids click around to games when they should be working. Tablets break and need expensive repairs. They reduce face-to-face interaction. We've replaced books with screens — and we shouldn't have. Bring back books in classrooms.

questions.question: Sylvia notices her dog Spot picks up his food dish when her alarm goes off. What would BEST test if Spot links the alarm to food?

Set the alarm at different times of day and see if Spot picks up his dish
Play a different alarm sound each day
questions.explanation: Change WHEN the alarm sounds. If Spot still picks up the dish, the alarm IS the cue.

wrongExplanations.3: Tests sound type, not the alarm itself.

What is the main idea of paragraph 2 specifically?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 is about arms acting on their own.
questions.passageText:
Octopuses have nine brains. One central brain coordinates the body, but each of its eight arms also has its own small brain.

This means each arm can solve problems on its own. Scientists have watched octopus arms open jars, untie knots, and even taste food independently.

This unusual nervous system gives octopuses special abilities — changing color in less than a second, slipping through tiny cracks, and using tools.

Studying octopus brains may help us understand intelligence in completely different ways than studying mammals.

questions.question: Why did Sara hug Mom "without saying anything"?
questions.passageText: It was 11pm. Mom usually came home from her shift at 10. Sara checked her phone — no messages. She tried to read but kept reading the same sentence. She got up and looked out the window. No headlights. She sat back down. The clock said 11:08. She tried calling. Voicemail. She walked to the kitchen, opened the fridge, closed it without taking anything. At 11:23, headlights swept across the wall. Sara ran to the door. Mom walked in, looking tired. "Sorry honey, train was delayed." Sara hugged her tightly without saying anything.

Octopuses have nine brains. One central brain coordinates the body, but each of its eight arms also has its own small brain.

This unusual nervous system gives octopuses special abilities — changing color in less than a second, slipping through tiny cracks, and using tools.

Studying octopus brains may help us understand intelligence in completely different ways than studying mammals.
